reading: Curve A: a single big-Nat multiply+compare is GMP-FLAT (≈O(1) kernel steps) even at ~10^5 digits — so per-op arithmetic cost is NOT the wall. Curve B: a flat conjunction of distinct big-Nat facts walls at K_max ≈ 400 within the 130s cap — confirming the killer is the NUMBER of distinct facts, not per-op cost. A sound DENSE PSD check states Ω(N²) distinct facts, so the largest dense block a packed/CRT check clears is N_max ≈ √K_max ≈ 20. Since 20 << 130–414, approach A/CRT does NOT break the dense wall — confirming ADR 0047's trust-model boundary, and SHARPENING it: the wall is distinct-fact-count Ω(N²), not arithmetic. The only escape is sub-Ω(N²) via STRUCTURE (Schur-tiling, probe 7b).
